articles.title: Dengue is no longer just a travel risk—what Google's mosquito plan could mean for your summer
articles.summary: This is not science fiction or some perverse prank. A Silicon Valley tech giant is seeking federal approval to release up to 64 million sterilized male mosquitoes in California and Florida over the next two years.

articles.title: Visual AI tracks nearly 100 wildlife species to improve conservation
articles.summary: Wildlife research projects worldwide could benefit from a new AI system which can automatically find, name, and follow individual animals in footage.

articles.title: Multiplexed, precise genome engineering in monocots with twin prime editing systems
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03174-5 Plant genome editing is multiplexed with twin prime editing.

articles.title: Structural motif search across the protein universe with Folddisco
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03162-9 Folddisco enables protein structural motif search in million scale databases.

articles.title: Novel synthetic biomolecule degrades disease-related proteins
articles.summary: Northwestern Medicine scientists have developed a novel synthetic biomolecular condensate that can degrade intracellular disease-causing proteins, providing a framework for new therapeutic approaches for a wide range of diseases, as detailed in a recent study published in Nature Communications.

articles.title: Marked for destruction
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03182-5 The significance of the first FDA-approved PROTAC lies not in proving that targeted protein degradation is superior to conventional therapies, but in proving that degrading proteins is possible as a therapeutic.

articles.title: AI offers promise for agriculture, but smallholder farmers risk being left behind
articles.summary: Globally, agriculture faces mounting pressures. These are driven by climate change, land degradation, labor shortages, supply chain disruptions and the demand for food from a growing population.

articles.title: Open-source software unlocks rapid DNA structure generation and analysis in one workflow
articles.summary: Computational chemists at the University of Amsterdam's Van 't Hoff Institute for Molecular Sciences have developed a comprehensive software suite to create accurate models of DNA in biomolecular assemblies. Called MDNA, the user-friendly molecular modeling toolkit helps biochemists, molecular biologists, bioinformaticians, and biophysicists to visualize and analyze DNA structures and perform accurate simulations.

articles.title: Armed with AI, study identifies prey from predator crunching sounds
articles.summary: Interactions between hard-shelled marine mollusks such as clams and snails and their predators play a critical but largely unseen role in shaping coastal ecosystems. These organisms help stabilize shorelines, filter water and support biodiversity, making them foundational to coastal health. Yet they are increasingly threatened by ocean acidification and expanding populations of mobile shell-crushing predators.

articles.title: Food industries embrace AI sensors to improve efficiencies
articles.summary: Food waste is a nagging problem that weighs heavily on global food production, distribution and sales industries—but an emerging generation of AI sensors is providing a raft of fresh solutions. The embrace of AI in food industries has been swift, which is why Flinders University researchers have worked with an international research team to build the first comprehensive overview of AI technologies involved in the food industry.

articles.title: Researcher who found Zcash's bug with AI adds Monero to his audit queue
articles.summary: Taylor Hornby, who uncovered the Orchard flaw that sent Zcash down 38%, says other privacy coins are on his list too.

articles.title: WLD plunges 20% as Hayes dumps token a day after saying he would keep holding it

articles.title: Bitcoin back above $61,000 after rout leads to $1.6 billion liquidations
articles.summary: Bitcoin fell as low as $59,227 overnight before recovering, steadying after Friday's strong jobs report set off a selloff that sank the Nasdaq 100 about 5% and rattled stocks, bonds and crypto together.

articles.title: Cardano social activity surges as ADA falls under 20 cents to four-year lows

articles.title: Travala lets AI agents book hotels with USDC on Base
articles.summary: Travala’s new protocol lets AI agents search and book hotels with USDC on Base, but travelers still approve the final payment.

articles.title: Crypto tax proposals weighed ahead of Tuesday House hearing
articles.summary: Among the issues US lawmakers are expected to discuss in a digital asset taxation hearing are “de minimis” reporting exceptions for crypto transactions.

articles.title: Hyperliquid treasuries stand alone in profit as legacy crypto DATs bleed billions
articles.summary: Strategy, Bitmine, and other major treasury firms have seen paper gains evaporate as crypto prices continue sliding.

articles.summary: Bitcoin bears piled into short positions as BTC price slid to $60,000. Will the $2.6 billion in short leverage lead to an upside squeeze?

articles.title: Why diehard bitcoin purists aren’t sweating the massive price crash that wiped out $200 billion
articles.summary: Mati Greenspan, Michael Saylor and Jameson Lopp blamed the AI boom for draining capital from bitcoin. Meanwhile, Jack Mallers refrained from sharing an outlook but recommended buying the dip.
